ground_truth.findings: Thorax is symmetric bilaterally. Mediastinum and trachea are midline. Lungs are fully expanded and clear. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. Adenopathy is present at the left hilus. Diaphragm and costophrenic angles appear normal. Bony structures are unremarkable. ET tube is in position. Nasogastric tube ends in the stomach.
ground_truth.impression: ET tube in position. Nasogastric tube ends in the stomach. No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality identified beyond adenopathy at the left hilus.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum is midline; trachea is central. A rounded calcified nodule is present in the posterior right lung base. No focal consolidation or abnormal opacities are seen in the lung fields. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits; possible decrease in right paratracheal prominence. Hila show a pretracheal lymph node conglomerate and right hilar lymph nodes. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles appear normal. Bony structures are unremarkable.
ground_truth.impression: No radiographic findings to suggest pneumonia. Consider correlation with CT findings for lymph node evaluation.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally but low in volume. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. Lung fields show calcified granuloma just below medial aspect of right hemidiaphragm; no focal airspace consolidation, pleural effusions, pulmonary edema, or pneumothorax. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. Hila are not enlarged. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles appear normal. Bony structures show inferior sternotomy wire fractured; surgical clips and vascular markers present related to prior CABG surgery. Lateral view somewhat limited due to overlying motion artifact.
ground_truth.impression: No evidence of acute cardiopulmonary process.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. Satisfactorily positioned endotracheal tube, left subclavian catheter, and nasogastric tube are present. Lung fields demonstrate slightly decreased volumes and possible slight interstitial edema; increased left basal atelectasis with slight right basal atelectasis and effusions not well seen. Pulmonary arteries are enlarged. Cardiac silhouette is unremarkable. Hila are unremarkable. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles appear normal. Bony structures are unremarkable.
ground_truth.impression: Increased mild pulmonary edema and left basal atelectasis. Consider pulmonary arterial hypertension evaluation based on enlarged pulmonary arteries.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline; tracheostomy tube in position. Left pleural effusion resolved; no pneumothorax identified. ground_truth.impression: 1. Left PICC tip terminates in the distal left brachiocephalic vein. 2. Mild pulmonary vascular congestion. 3. Residual streaky opacity likely atelectasis. Left pleural effusion resolved.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. The right lower lobe demonstrates atelectasis. The left lower lobe opacity is increased. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles are unremarkable. Bony structures are unremarkable. NG tube tip is below the diaphragm. ET tube is in position. Right IJ catheter tip is at the confluence of the brachiocephalic vein.
ground_truth.impression: Left lower lobe atelectasis increased. Opacities superior to the hila bilaterally, larger on the left. Consider evaluation for underlying causes such as infection, fluid, or other pathology.
